privacy.meta_desc: Privacy policy and data protection at Instituto Milhomem, in compliance with the Brazilian LGPD.
privacy.last_updated: Last updated: April 2025 · In compliance with
privacy.s1_p1: Instituto Milhomem (CNPJ: 39.875.746/0001-84), headquartered in Setor Bueno, Goiânia – GO, is the controller of personal data processed on this website, pursuant to art. 5, VI of the LGPD.
privacy.s1_p2: Our contact channel for privacy matters:
privacy.s2_intro: We collect only the data necessary for the purposes described below:
privacy.s2_b1_text: name, email, phone, service type, and optional message.
privacy.s2_b2_text: IP address, browser type, pages visited and time spent (via essential technical cookies, no third-party tracking).
privacy.s2_b3_text: access credentials (email and encrypted password) of authorized staff.
privacy.s2_note: We do not collect sensitive data (health, biometric, etc.) without explicit consent.
privacy.s3_b1: Respond to inquiries and schedule treatments.
privacy.s3_b2: Send communications related to the requested service.
privacy.s3_b3: Improve the website experience and performance.
privacy.s3_b4: Comply with legal and regulatory obligations.
privacy.s3_legal_text: consent (art. 7, I), contract performance (art. 7, V) and legitimate interest (art. 7, IX), as applicable to each purpose.
privacy.s4_intro: Your data is not sold or shared with third parties for commercial purposes. We may share it exclusively with:
privacy.s4_b1: IT service providers (hosting, email) under confidentiality agreements.
privacy.s4_b2: Public authorities, when required by law.
privacy.s5_p1: We use only essential technical cookies required for the website to function (session maintenance, security). We do not use tracking or advertising cookies.
privacy.s5_p2: You can disable cookies in your browser settings, but this may affect some website features.
privacy.s6_p1: Contact form data is kept for the period necessary to fulfill the request and subsequently for the legal period of 5 years for potential proof of service delivery (art. 12 of the Brazilian Consumer Protection Code).
privacy.s6_p2: Staff data is deleted within 30 days of contract termination.
privacy.s7_b1: Confirm the existence of processing of your personal data.
privacy.s7_b2: Access, correct or update your data.
privacy.s7_b3: Request anonymization, blocking or deletion of unnecessary data.
privacy.s7_b5: File a complaint with the National Data Protection Authority (ANPD).
privacy.s7_contact_pre: To exercise your rights, contact us at
privacy.s7_contact_post: with the subject "LGPD – [your right]". We will respond within 15 days.
privacy.s8_p1: We adopt technical and organizational measures to protect your data, including: password encryption (bcrypt), HTTPS communication, role-based access control (RBAC) and audit logs of administrative actions.
privacy.s9_p1: This policy may be updated periodically. Significant changes will be communicated via a notice on the website. The date of the last update is always indicated at the top of this page.

terms.meta_desc: Terms and conditions of use for the Instituto Milhomem website.
terms.s1_p1_pre: By accessing and using the website
terms.s1_p1_mid: ("Website"), you agree to these Terms of Use and our
terms.s1_p1_post: . If you do not agree, please do not use the Website.
terms.s2_p1: The Website has an informational and commercial purpose, presenting the hair transplant services and procedures offered by Instituto Milhomem. The content provided does not constitute individualized medical advice.
terms.s2_p2: For diagnosis and treatment recommendations, please consult a qualified professional at Instituto Milhomem in person.
terms.s3_intro: You may use the Website to:
terms.s3_b1: Obtain information about the services offered.
terms.s3_b2: Contact us and request a consultation appointment.
terms.s4_b1: Reproduce, copy or distribute Website content without prior written authorization.
terms.s4_b2: Use the Website to send spam, phishing or engage in any illegal activity.
terms.s4_b3: Attempt to access restricted areas without authorization (e.g. admin panel).
terms.s4_b4: Use bots, scrapers or any automated means to collect data from the Website.
terms.s4_b5: Post false, defamatory content or content that violates third-party rights.
terms.s5_p1: All Website content — texts, images, logos, videos and source code — is the property of Instituto Milhomem or its licensors, protected by Brazilian Copyright Law No. 9,610/1998.
terms.s5_p2: Partial or total reproduction without express authorization constitutes copyright infringement and subjects the offender to applicable legal penalties.
terms.s6_p1: Information about medical procedures, results and treatments available on the Website is for educational and informational purposes only.
terms.s6_p2: Individual results may vary. Instituto Milhomem does not guarantee specific results. All therapeutic recommendations are made individually during an in-person medical consultation.
terms.s7_p1: The Website may contain links to third-party websites (e.g. social media, scientific publications). Instituto Milhomem is not responsible for the content, privacy policies or practices of those websites.
terms.s8_p1: Instituto Milhomem shall not be liable for direct, indirect, incidental or consequential damages arising from the use or inability to use the Website, including technical interruptions, content errors or data loss.
terms.s9_p1: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Changes take effect upon publication. Continued use of the Website after changes implies acceptance of the new Terms.
terms.s10_p1: These Terms are governed by Brazilian law. Any disputes shall be resolved in the courts of Goiânia – GO, waiving any other jurisdiction, however privileged it may be.
terms.s11_p1: Questions about these Terms? Contact us:
